progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Keep knees tracking over toes; do not let them cave inward.
safetyNotes: Control depth and keep knees tracking over the toes; brace your core.
whySelected: Best-loaded home squat; goblet position keeps the torso upright and is forgiving for beginners.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: reduce depth or hold lighter dumbbells.
jointRiskNotes: Knee and lower-back focus — control depth; reduce range if the knees ache.
safetyNotes: Control depth and keep knees tracking over the toes; brace your core.
whySelected: Dumbbells at sides allow heavier loading than goblet once stronger.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: hold a doorframe/chair for more support.
jointRiskNotes: Knee and lower-back focus — control depth; reduce range if the knees ache.
safetyNotes: Control depth and keep knees tracking over the toes; brace your core.
whySelected: Beginner entry point; external support reduces balance demand on low-energy days.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: shorten the stance and hold support.
jointRiskNotes: Front knee stays behind toes-ish; control the descent.
safetyNotes: Control depth and keep knees tracking over the toes; brace your core.
whySelected: Unilateral strength + balance with no equipment; bridges to lunges.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: use a lighter band or partial range.
jointRiskNotes: Knee and lower-back focus — control depth; reduce range if the knees ache.
safetyNotes: Control depth and keep knees tracking over the toes; brace your core.
whySelected: Band-only squat for travel/no-dumbbell days; adds resistance at the top.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Hinge at the hips with a flat back; do not round the lower spine.
safetyNotes: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine; brace and don't round the lower back.
whySelected: Primary posterior-chain builder (hamstrings/glutes); the key hinge pattern.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Flat back throughout; load stays close to the legs.
safetyNotes: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine; brace and don't round the lower back.
whySelected: Hamstring-biased hinge variation; canonical over near-duplicates.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: elevate the dumbbells on a low surface to reduce range.
jointRiskNotes: Brace the core; hips and shoulders rise together.
safetyNotes: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine; brace and don't round the lower back.
whySelected: Full hip hinge from the floor; complements RDL with more quad/glute.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back focus — keep it neutral; stop if you feel lumbar strain.
safetyNotes: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine; brace and don't round the lower back.
whySelected: Band hinge for low-load/low-energy or dumbbell-free sessions.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Step length controls knee stress; avoid knee collapsing inward.
safetyNotes: Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the foot.
whySelected: Loaded unilateral leg work; strong carryover to daily movement.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Reverse step is gentler on the front knee than forward lunges.
safetyNotes: Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the foot.
whySelected: Knee-friendlier lunge variation; good default for beginners with knee caution.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Knee focus — keep the front knee stable; reverse lunges are gentler on the knee.
safetyNotes: Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the foot.
whySelected: Bodyweight progression option; adds a light conditioning element.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Knee focus — keep the front knee stable; reverse lunges are gentler on the knee.
safetyNotes: Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the foot.
whySelected: Bodyweight beginner lunge; usable on low-energy days.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Opposite-side load adds a light anti-rotation core demand.
safetyNotes: Lower under control and keep the front knee tracking over the foot.
whySelected: Lunge + core integration; one loaded carry-over option.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: incline push-up (hands on a counter) or knees.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder/elbow focus — floor variations limit risky end-range.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulder blades set and lower under control; don't flare the elbows hard.
whySelected: Foundational bodyweight horizontal push; scales endlessly.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder/elbow focus — floor variations limit risky end-range.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulder blades set and lower under control; don't flare the elbows hard.
whySelected: Beginner push regression with triceps emphasis; low-energy friendly.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder/elbow focus — floor variations limit risky end-range.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulder blades set and lower under control; don't flare the elbows hard.
whySelected: Harder push progression emphasizing triceps/inner chest.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: lighter load; pause on the floor each rep.
jointRiskNotes: Floor limits shoulder extension — shoulder-friendly pressing.
safetyNotes: Performed lying on the floor; no bench needed.
whySelected: Loaded horizontal press without a bench; shoulder-safe floor variant.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: reduce load and range; keep a soft elbow bend.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder focus — the floor caps the range; don't over-stretch at the bottom.
safetyNotes: Performed lying on the FLOOR (not a bench); keep elbows lightly bent throughout.
whySelected: Chest isolation/stretch to complement pressing; floor version limits risky shoulder end-range.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder/elbow focus — floor variations limit risky end-range.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulder blades set and lower under control; don't flare the elbows hard.
whySelected: Adds band tension to the push-up for a strength-day option without dumbbells.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: press one arm at a time or reduce load.
jointRiskNotes: Press slightly in front of the head; avoid forcing end-range overhead.
safetyNotes: Use a normal chair with back support — no bench required.
whySelected: Beginner overhead press with back support; the canonical seated press.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Brace the core so the lower back doesn't arch.
safetyNotes: Keep ribs down and core braced; press without arching the lower back.
whySelected: Standing press adds full-body bracing; main vertical push.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder focus — rotate smoothly; skip if it pinches the shoulder.
safetyNotes: Use a stable chair with back support; no bench required. Rotate the wrists smoothly and keep ribs down.
whySelected: Delt variation with more front-delt and rotation; canonical over its V.2 twin.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder focus — don't force end-range overhead; stop on any pinch.
safetyNotes: Keep ribs down and core braced; press without arching the lower back.
whySelected: Band overhead press for low-load/low-energy days.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: support chest on thighs (more upright) and lighten.
jointRiskNotes: Flat back, hinge at hips; don't round the spine under load.
safetyNotes: Keep a neutral spine and pull with the back — don't jerk the weight up.
whySelected: Primary horizontal pull and the backbone of back work without a bar.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: brace free hand on thigh/knee (not a bench), lighten.
jointRiskNotes: Self-supported on your own leg — no bench. Keep spine neutral.
safetyNotes: Performed self-supported; do NOT require a bench.
whySelected: Heavier per-arm rowing with a free-standing brace; bench-free by design.

displayName: Dumbbell Palm Rotational Bent Over Row
progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back/shoulder focus — hinge with a flat back, don't round.
safetyNotes: Keep a neutral spine and pull with the back — don't jerk the weight up.
whySelected: Row variation that adds biceps/grip variety to back days.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back/shoulder focus — hinge with a flat back, don't round.
safetyNotes: Sit on the floor and loop the band around your own feet — no door or wall anchor. Keep a tall, neutral spine.
whySelected: Beginner, low-energy band row that anchors around your feet; replaces the door-dependent towel row.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back/elbow focus — flat back; underhand grip adds biceps emphasis.
safetyNotes: Keep a neutral spine and pull with the back — don't jerk the weight up.
whySelected: Underhand bent-over row for lat/biceps variety; fully free-standing, no anchor needed.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: reduce range (stop at forehead level) and load.
jointRiskNotes: Floor caps shoulder flexion. Skip if it causes any shoulder pinch.
safetyNotes: Lying on the FLOOR (not a bench), which safely limits the overhead range.
whySelected: Closest safe lat-stretch movement at home; floor version limits risky end-range.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder focus — keep it controlled; skip if it pinches.
safetyNotes: Move with control and squeeze the shoulder blades; avoid shrugging or swinging.
whySelected: Upper-back/rear-delt row for posture; offsets all the pressing volume.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Lead with the elbows; avoid shrugging.
safetyNotes: Move with control and squeeze the shoulder blades; avoid shrugging or swinging.
whySelected: Rear-delt/scapular-retraction isolation — key for shoulder balance at home.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Shoulder focus — keep it controlled; skip if it pinches.
safetyNotes: Band held in both hands at chest height (no anchor needed).
whySelected: Anchor-free band back work; great low-energy upper-back option.

progression: Add 5 seconds per set each session; once you hold the top of the range for all sets, add a set or progress to a harder variation.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back focus — keep it flat, not arched.
safetyNotes: Time-based hold (seconds). Keep hips level; don't let the lower back sag.
whySelected: Foundational anti-extension core; teaches bracing for hinges/squats.

progression: Add 5 seconds per set each session; once you hold the top of the range for all sets, add a set or progress to a harder variation.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back focus — keep it flat, not arched.
safetyNotes: Time-based hold (seconds).
whySelected: Plank variation for progression variety.

progression: Add 5 seconds per set each session; once you hold the top of the range for all sets, add a set or progress to a harder variation.
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back focus — brace and avoid twisting under fatigue.
safetyNotes: Time-based hold each side. Resists lateral flexion/rotation — best anti-rotation proxy without an anchor.
whySelected: Trains rotary/lateral stability; stands in for pallof presses, which need a mounted anchor we don't assume.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
regression: move only the legs (keep arms still).
jointRiskNotes: Lower-back focus — brace and avoid twisting under fatigue.
safetyNotes: Keep the lower back pressed to the floor; contralateral pattern resists trunk rotation.
whySelected: Anti-rotation/rotary stability with contralateral limbs; very joint-friendly and doubles as anti-extension.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Neck/lower-back focus — support the head; avoid yanking the neck.
safetyNotes: Curl the trunk; don't yank the neck.
whySelected: Basic trunk flexion; accessible beginner ab work.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Neck/lower-back focus — support the head; avoid yanking the neck.
safetyNotes: Curl the trunk smoothly and don't pull on your neck.
whySelected: Adds oblique emphasis to flexion work.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Keep the lower back down; if it arches, bend the knees.
safetyNotes: Curl the trunk smoothly and don't pull on your neck.
whySelected: Lower-ab focused flexion; band adds optional resistance.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Neck/lower-back focus — support the head; avoid yanking the neck.
safetyNotes: Curl the trunk smoothly and don't pull on your neck.
whySelected: Longer-range flexion progression beyond the crunch.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Low joint risk when performed with control.
safetyNotes: Drive through the heels and squeeze the glutes; keep ribs down.
whySelected: Foundational glute activation; safe for everyone and great on low-energy days.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Low joint risk when performed with control.
safetyNotes: Keep hips level as each foot lifts.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

safetyNotes: Band around the hips (held/looped), no fixed anchor.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.
jointRiskNotes: Low joint risk when performed with control.
safetyNotes: Drive through the heels and squeeze the glutes; keep ribs down.
whySelected: Glute-medius/abductor work for hip stability and knee tracking.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

safetyNotes: Use a full, controlled range; pause at the top and lower slowly.

safetyNotes: Use a full, controlled range; pause at the top and lower slowly.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

safetyNotes: Seated on a normal chair; dumbbells rest on the knees.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

safetyNotes: Brace the elbow on the inner thigh (seated on a chair) — no bench.

progression: Add 1–2 reps per set each session; once you hit the top of the rep range for all sets at the target RPE, add a small load (or a set) and drop back to the bottom of the range.

safetyNotes: Seated on a normal chair.
